items.front: Terminal arrival strip block 3
items.back: Number of aircraft if more than one, wake category indicator, type of aircraft, and aircraft equipment suffix.

items.front: Terminal arrival strip block 5
items.back: Secondary radar/beacon code assigned.

items.front: Terminal arrival strip block 8
items.back: Estimated time of arrival at the coordination fix or destination airport.

items.front: Terminal arrival strip block 9A: protected item
items.back: Minimum fuel may not be omitted by facility directive.

items.front: Branch prefix VM
items.back: U.S. Marine Corps.

items.front: Branch prefix VV
items.back: U.S. Navy.

items.front: Mission prefix RCH
items.back: AMC, Air Mobility Command.

items.front: Mission prefix F
items.back: Flight Check.

items.front: Commercial aircraft carrying the President
items.back: Use EXEC1.

items.front: Commercial aircraft carrying the Vice President's family
items.back: Use EXEC2F.

items.front: Frequency 126.55
items.back: One two six point five five.

items.front: Frequency 275.8
items.back: Two seven five point eight.

items.front: Heading 005
items.back: Heading zero zero five.

items.front: Beacon code 1000
items.back: One zero zero zero.

items.front: Flight level 180
items.back: Flight level one eight zero.

items.front: Altimeter 30.01
items.back: Altimeter three zero zero one.

items.front: Five-step taxiway lights: night, visibility less than 1 mile
items.back: Step 4.

items.front: Five-step taxiway lights: night, visibility 1 mile or more
items.back: Step 3.

items.front: Three-step taxiway lights: day, visibility less than 1 mile
items.back: Step 3.

items.front: One-step taxiway lights: night
items.back: On.

items.front: Intersecting runway departure: preceding departure release condition
items.back: Do not begin takeoff roll until the preceding aircraft has departed and passed the intersection or is turning to avert conflict.

items.front: Intersecting runway departure: preceding arrival release conditions
items.back: The preceding arrival must be clear of the landing runway, have completed landing roll with an acknowledged hold-short/exit instruction, be observed turning before the intersection, or have passed the intersection.

items.front: Intersecting runway departure wake: B-I behind Category A
items.back: 3 minutes if flight paths will cross.

items.front: Intersecting runway departure wake: pilot deviation request
items.back: Do not approve deviation from required time intervals if the preceding aircraft requires wake turbulence separation.

items.front: Same runway arrival: landed aircraft, Category I behind Category I or II
items.back: 3,000 feet from the landing threshold if suitable landmarks determine distance.

items.front: Same runway arrival: landed aircraft, Category II behind Category I or II
items.back: 4,500 feet from the landing threshold if suitable landmarks determine distance.

items.front: Same runway arrival: departed aircraft airborne, either Category III
items.back: 6,000 feet from the landing threshold if suitable landmarks determine distance.

items.front: Same runway arrival: succeeding helicopter or powered-lift
items.back: Visual separation may be applied instead of distance minima.

items.front: Intersecting runway arrival: preceding departure
items.back: Arrival must not cross the landing threshold/flight path until the departure has passed the intersection/flight path or is airborne and turning to avert conflict.

items.front: Intersecting runway arrival: preceding arrival
items.back: The preceding arrival must be clear of the landing runway, have completed landing roll and will hold short, or have passed the intersection/flight path.

items.front: LAHSO: traffic information and acknowledgment
items.back: Issue traffic information to both aircraft and obtain acknowledgment from each no later than landing clearance.

items.front: Landing behind departure on crossing runway: Category I behind Category E
items.back: 2 minutes if the arrival will fly through the airborne path of the departure.

items.front: Terminal single sensor ASR/DTAS: less than 40 miles from antenna
items.back: 3 miles.

items.front: Terminal single sensor ASR/DTAS: 40 miles or more from antenna
items.back: 5 miles.

items.front: Terminal fusion target symbol
items.back: 3 miles.

items.front: Terminal fusion displaying ISR in data block
items.back: 5 miles.

items.front: ERAM below FL 600
items.back: 5 miles.

items.front: ERAM at or above FL 600
items.back: 10 miles.

items.front: Arrival instructions before approach gate: required information
items.back: Position relative to final-course fix/NAVAID/airport, vector to intercept if required, approach clearance when eligible, and frequency/monitor/contact instructions.

items.front: Approach clearance after vectoring to published segment
items.back: Assign an altitude to maintain until established on the segment of a published route or instrument approach procedure.

items.front: TAA-supported approach clearance
items.back: Inform the aircraft of its position relative to the appropriate IAF and issue the approach clearance.

items.front: Adjacent airport terminal wake: Category B-I behind Category A
items.back: 3 minutes when the IFR aircraft course will cross the flight path.

items.front: Adjacent airport terminal wake: Category B-I behind Category B or D
items.back: 2 minutes when the IFR aircraft course will cross the flight path.

items.front: Adjacent airport en route wake: heavy/large/small behind super
items.back: 3 minutes when the IFR aircraft course will cross the flight path.

items.front: Adjacent airport en route wake: small behind B757
items.back: 2 minutes when the IFR aircraft course will cross the flight path.

items.front: Same-course departures: following aircraft climbs through leading aircraft's assigned altitude
items.back: Use 3 minutes until the following aircraft passes through the leading aircraft's assigned altitude.

items.front: Same-course departures: DME/ATD distance alternative
items.back: Use 5 miles between DME-equipped aircraft, RNAV aircraft using ATD, or DME/ATD aircraft when the DME aircraft is 10,000 feet or below or outside 10 miles from the DME NAVAID.

items.front: Diverging radials: minimum divergence
items.back: At least 15 degrees, with either aircraft clear of the protected airspace for the other aircraft.

items.front: Non-DME divergence table: 25 degrees
items.back: 10 NM.

items.front: DME divergence table: 30 degrees below FL 180
items.back: 9 NM.

items.front: DME divergence table: 30 degrees FL 180 through FL 450
items.back: 11 NM.

items.front: Timed approach sequence: succeeding aircraft clearance
items.back: Clear it for approach, to descend to the altitude vacated by the preceding aircraft, and to leave the FAF/outer marker/substitute fix inbound at a specified time.

items.front: Timed approach sequence after aircraft leave holding/approach fix inbound
items.back: Vertical separation is no longer provided; longitudinal separation is used.

items.front: CPDLC response attribute W/U
items.back: Response required; valid responses include WILCO, UNABLE, STANDBY, NOT CURRENT DATA AUTHORITY, NOT AUTHORIZED NEXT DATA AUTHORITY, logical acknowledgement if required, and ERROR.

items.front: CPDLC response attribute N
items.back: No response required unless logical acknowledgement is required.

items.front: CPDLC failure on a specific aircraft
items.back: Send a connection request message (CR1) or advise the pilot via backup communications to log on again.
